title: Scenario 08 Stale Validity / Authority Revocation Boundary Delta Record

source_record.prior_record_context: Fork-preserved record created or relied on inside an earlier validity window

boundary_sequence.claim_state_after_transition: The prior record remains inspectable as evidence of the T1 state; it does not establish current authority outside the recorded validity conditions.
A revocation, expiry, policy change, evidence supersession, role change, purpose narrowing, or environment change requires current revalidation.
The downstream actor attempts to treat prior validity as current validity; the preserved record does not support that inference.

supported_after_transition: The prior record can be inspected as a bounded T1 record.
The validity-changing event can be inspected.
The downstream stale-validity reliance attempt can be recorded.
Required current revalidation can be preserved.
fork_role: Fork records stale-validity and revocation boundary state without transferring authority, approving current reliance, determining compliance, determining admissibility, establishing legal sufficiency, deciding acceptance, or judging correctness.
limitations: This BDR records temporal validity boundary state only.
This BDR does not decide whether the prior claim was true or correct.
This BDR does not decide whether current reliance is allowed.
Current reliance requires separate current authority, evidence, policy, role, purpose, and environment support.
